name: Sierra de Aracena Natural Park Loop
name_original: Los Marines y el Castillo de Aracena desde Fuente de León — circular por las montañas del Parque Natural de la Sierra de Aracena
description: A difficult 60.6-mile road cycling route through the Sierra de Aracena mountains, featuring Aracena Castle and scenic viewpoints.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Fuentes De León traverse a diverse landscape in the south of Badajoz province, Spain. The region features rolling hills, holm oak and olive groves, and varied topography with elevations ranging from 200 to 741 meters above sea level. This environment provides a mix of ascents and descents, offering engaging rides through Mediterranean flora. The area's natural features contribute to a scenic backdrop for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Fuentes De León The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Aracena Natural Park Loop, a 60.6 miles (97.6 km) trail that takes 6 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views through natural parkland. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Church of Saint James the Apostle – Santiaguista Convent loop from Cabeza la Vaca, a difficult 49.5 miles (79.6 km) path. This route winds through the characteristic landscapes of the region, passing historical landmarks. Local road cyclists also love the Monastery of Tentudía – Tentudía Summit loop from Segura de León, a 46.6 miles (75.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Fuentes De León is defined by rolling hills, holm oak and olive groves, and varied topography. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Fuentes De León's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Fuentes De León?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Fuentes De León listed on komoot, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Fuentes De León?
A: The region is characterized by rolling hills, holm oak and olive groves, and varied topography. Elevations range from 200 to 741 meters above sea level, providing a mix of ascents and descents through Mediterranean flora.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Fuentes De León?
A: While many routes are classified as moderate or difficult, the varied topography means there are sections and shorter loops that can be enjoyed by less experienced riders. It's advisable to check the elevation profile of a route before setting out. Currently, there are 12 moderate routes available.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenges, with 29 routes classified as difficult. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992453975/sierra-de-aracena-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Aracena Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 60.6-mile (97.6 km) route with over 2,000 meters of elevation gain, offering extensive views through natural parkland.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Fuentes De León are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint James the Apostle – Santiaguista Convent loop from Cabeza la Vaca&lt;/a&gt;, a 49.5-mile (79.6 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Tentudía – Tentudía Summit loop from Segura de León&lt;/a&gt;, which is 46.6 miles (75.0 km) long.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The region offers several notable attractions. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Fuentes de León&lt;/a&gt;, a Natural Monument, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4093491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Tentudía&lt;/a&gt;. Scenic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5343020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Puerto Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offer magnificent vistas over the valleys and surrounding Sierra.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Fuentes De León?
A: The Mediterranean climate suggests spring and autumn are ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally mild but may have cooler, wetter days. The region's natural beauty, with holm oaks and cork oaks, is enjoyable across seasons.

Q: Is Fuentes De León a good destination for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, families can find quieter roads and shorter sections to enjoy. The serene environment, away from heavy traffic, makes it pleasant for cycling. It's recommended to choose routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances for family outings.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or stay after a ride in Fuentes De León?
A: Fuentes de León and nearby towns offer various options for refreshments and accommodation. You'll find local cafes and restaurants serving traditional Extremaduran cuisine, as well as guesthouses and hotels suitable for cyclists looking to relax after a day on the road.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options near Fuentes De León?
A: Yes, the region provides opportunities for longer rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992453975/sierra-de-aracena-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Aracena Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at over 60 miles (97 km), is a significant long-distance option that takes you through extensive natural parkland and offers challenging climbs.

Q: What are the parking options for cyclists in Fuentes De León?
A: Parking is generally available in the town of Fuentes de León. As a smaller municipality, you can typically find street parking or designated areas, especially near the town center, which can serve as a convenient starting point for your cycling adventures.

Q: Can I reach Fuentes De León by public transport for cycling?
A: Fuentes de León is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger towns and cities in the Badajoz province. However, options might be limited, so it's advisable to check schedules in advance and confirm if bicycles can be transported.

Q: What makes road cycling in Fuentes De León unique?
A: The unique appeal lies in its diverse landscapes, ranging from rolling hills to holm oak and olive groves, combined with significant natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves of Fuentes de León&lt;/a&gt;. The varied topography ensures engaging rides with a blend of climbs and descents, all set within a tranquil Mediterranean ecosystem.
tips: The town of Cortelacía is home to the Parroquia Nuestra Señora de Los Remedios, which is located in the main square where the town hall is also located. The center is in the old middle of the Picos de Aroche.

The name of Los Marines has a curious history: during a visit by King Felipe II to the municipality, an inhabitant asked him why, being so powerful, he did not bring the sea to the town. The king's response was: "I will not bring the sea, but you will be sailors." Since then, all the young people of the town performed their military service in Marina Huelva.
This picturesque municipality, surrounded by nature and beauty, is an ideal place to explore during a getaway to the Sierra de Huelva. Don't hesitate to visit Los Marines and enjoy its charm.

The Hermitage of the Queen of the Angels is a Catholic temple located in the Peña de Arias Montano, belonging to the town of Alájar. Every September 8, it hosts the pilgrimage of the Queen of the Angels, the one that summons the most pilgrims in the Sierra de Aracena region.

The town of Aracena is dominated by its castle, where it is located on a hill and where you can see the group of houses and surroundings that surround it. The Castle of Aracena is accompanied by the Iglesia del Castillo.

Lookout point from which you can see the entire Sierra de Cuchillar mountains and the valley through which the Rivera de Huelva passes. To get to the viewpoint, you have to walk about a hundred meters along an unpaved track.

faqs.question: How challenging is the road cycling route from Fuente de León to Los Marines and the Castillo de Aracena?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult road cycling route, spanning nearly 98 km with over 2,000 meters of elevation gain. The Sierra de Aracena is characterized by hilly and rugged terrain, making it physically demanding. It requires very good fitness and is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the estimated duration for completing this circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this circular road cycling route is approximately 6 hours and 11 minutes, not including stops for rest or sightseeing.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the natural beauty of the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Aracena, passing through luxuriant forests of holm oaks, cork oaks, and chestnuts, as well as spectacular pastureland. The route also takes you through picturesque 'white villages' with traditional architecture, and offers panoramic views from the Aracena Castle.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass through charming villages like Los Marines Village and see the Parish of Our Lady of Remedies, Cortelazor. Key highlights include the Aracena Castle and the Arias Montano Peak and the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Angels, both offering stunning views. You'll also find the Castañuelo Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car near the starting point in Fuente de León?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for Fuente de León are not available, villages in the Sierra de Aracena typically have public parking areas. It's advisable to look for designated parking within Fuente de León or nearby Aracena, especially if you plan to start early.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Aracena is particularly verdant in spring with wildflowers and vibrant in autumn with changing foliage. These seasons generally offer more pleasant temperatures for cycling than the peak of summer. However, the region receives more rain due to Atlantic winds, so be prepared for varied weather.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for cycling in the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Aracena?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for road cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of private property. For detailed information, you can consult the official Visit Andalucía website or local tourism offices.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling, primarily on paved public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a long, difficult road cycling route like this is not practical or safe for the animal. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this particular adventure.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino de Santiago del Sur, the [CIMA HL06] Sierra del Viento * Rivera de Hinojales, and the [CIMA HL03] Aracena, among others. The region has an extensive network of public roads connecting villages, making it a hub for various routes.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for food and drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Sierra de Aracena is renowned for its gastronomy, especially Iberian ham. The route passes through several charming villages, including Los Marines and Aracena, where you'll find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel and sample local delicacies. Taking breaks in these villages is a great way to experience the local culture.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult road cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire for varying weather conditions, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A GPS device or a printed map is also highly recommended, as some segments might be less clearly marked.

waypoints.name: Parish of Our Lady of Remedies, Cortelazor
Arias Montano Peak and the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Angels
